name: Taste Skill
analogy: Like hiring a design director who sits behind every AI coder — they won't let anything generic, boring, or sloppy get shipped.
whatItDoes: A set of design rule files you drop into any project. When AI generates frontend code, it follows these rules to create premium-looking interfaces instead of generic templates. Covers typography, colors, layout, animations, and anti-patterns.
whyYouUseIt: Your projects like DevToolkit and SafetyIQ use AI to generate UI code. Without taste-skill, the AI defaults to boring Inter font, centered layouts, and purple gradients. With it, you get Geist fonts, emerald accents, staggered animations, and polished hover states automatically.
whenToSwap: If you want a completely custom design system with a dedicated designer, you wouldn't need this. But for solo developers using AI to build UIs, this is the best way to get consistent quality.
